title: Connection Pool Exhaustion — Black Friday Collapse
summary: Production database accepting zero new connections at peak load. 2.3M active users locked out during the highest-traffic day of the year. Revenue bleeding at $8K/min.
rootCause: PgBouncer pool_size=100 not scaled for 8× holiday traffic surge; idle connections held until 60s timeout, starving new requests.
resolution: Emergency: pool_size → 800, max_client_conn → 2000, server_idle_timeout → 30s. Post-mortem: autoscaling pool config + load-test suite added to CI.

title: Silent Data Corruption via Race Condition
summary: Audit surfaced 0.3% of financial ledger records with wrong account IDs — caused by non-atomic read-modify-write in concurrent transaction handler that passed all unit tests.
rootCause: SELECT + UPDATE without row-level lock allowed two concurrent transactions to read the same stale balance, then both commit divergent states.
resolution: Wrapped all balance mutations in SELECT FOR UPDATE with serializable isolation. Added idempotency keys. Wrote and ran reconciliation script to fix 14,000 corrupted records.

title: API Gateway Memory Leak — Silent OOM Crash Loop
summary: Every 72 hours the API gateway OOM-crashed. Response times degraded 10× in the hours before death. 50K RPS service dropping to zero with no prior alerting.
rootCause: Event listener registered per-request inside a closure that captured a 200KB buffer, preventing GC. Heap grew 200MB/hr.
resolution: Identified via Node.js heap snapshots. Moved listener to module scope. Added heap-usage alerting at 70% + graceful restart policy.

title: N+1 Query Catastrophe at 100× Scale
summary: A single dashboard page triggering 12,000+ SQL queries per request as the user table crossed 1M rows. Worked fine in staging. Brought the DB to its knees in prod.
rootCause: ORM lazy-loading nested relationships: for each of 500 users, it loaded 24 related records individually instead of a single JOIN.
resolution: Added eager loading with explicit JOIN strategy + query analyzer check in CI that fails on any query count > 20 per endpoint.

title: CDN Cache Key Poisoning — Cross-User Data Bleed
summary: Users occasionally seeing other users' private dashboard data. CDN cached authenticated responses and served them to unrelated users due to a missing Vary header.
rootCause: Authorization header excluded from CDN cache key. User A's authenticated response was cached and served to User B on the same CDN edge.
resolution: Added Vary: Authorization. Implemented user-scoped cache key strategy. Purged all affected CDN edges globally within 8 minutes.
